producedFindings.title: Semantic change: Remarks assigned to Description instead of Summary
producedFindings.description: The code was changed from `operation.Description = remarks;` to `operation.Summary = remarks;`. This changes the semantic meaning of where XML comment remarks are applied. If remarks were previously intended to populate the Description field, this change alters that behavior. The PR does not explain the rationale for this change, making it unclear if this is intentional or a bug.
producedFindings.recommendation: Clarify the intent: if remarks should go to Summary, verify this is correct per OpenAPI spec semantics. If this was unintentional, revert to Description. Add a comment explaining why remarks map to Summary rather than Description.

producedFindings.title: Parameter matching logic changed from metadata.Name to propertyName
producedFindings.description: The parameter matching logic was changed from `p.Name == metadata.Name` to `p.Name == propertyName`. While propertyName is extracted from metadata.PropertyName, this changes the matching criteria. If metadata.Name and propertyName differ (e.g., due to naming conventions or binding attributes), this could cause parameters to not be matched correctly, leading to missing descriptions.
producedFindings.recommendation: Verify that propertyName is the correct field to match against operation parameters. If metadata.Name was intentional, document why the change was made. Consider adding a test case that validates parameter matching with different naming scenarios.

producedFindings.title: Null check added for metadata but logic flow may be affected
producedFindings.description: A null check `metadata is not null` was added before accessing metadata.MetadataKind. This is a good defensive programming practice, but the change means that if metadata is null, the entire block is skipped. This could silently skip parameter documentation that should have been applied. Verify that null metadata is expected and should result in skipping the block.
producedFindings.recommendation: Confirm that null metadata should result in skipping parameter documentation. If metadata can be null but should still be processed, adjust the logic accordingly. Add logging or comments explaining when metadata might be null.

producedFindings.title: RequestBody.Description assignment reordered after example processing
producedFindings.description: The line `operation.RequestBody.Description = description;` was moved from line 467 to line 479 (after example processing). While this doesn't change functionality, it changes the order of operations. If example processing modifies the RequestBody or its Description, this reordering could affect behavior. The change appears to be stylistic but lacks explanation.
producedFindings.recommendation: Add a comment explaining why Description assignment was moved after example processing. Verify that this reordering doesn't affect the final state of RequestBody when both description and examples are present.

producedFindings.title: License header removed from generated code file
producedFindings.description: The .NET Foundation license header was removed from the top of XmlCommentGenerator.Emitter.cs. This file appears to be part of the ASP.NET Core project which typically includes license headers. Removing it may violate project conventions or licensing requirements.
producedFindings.recommendation: Restore the license header to maintain consistency with project conventions. If the header removal was intentional, document the reason and ensure it complies with project licensing policies.

groundTruth.category: All C# Source Files Must Include MIT License Header
groundTruth.title: Missing MIT license header
groundTruth.description: The rule requires that every C# source file must start with the exact two-line MIT license header comment: '// Licensed to the .NET Foundation under one or more agreements.' followed by '// The .NET Foundation licenses this file to you under the MIT license.' This file is missing the required license header at the start.

groundTruth.title: Remarks overwrite Summary instead of Description in XML comment transformation
groundTruth.description: When XML remarks are present, they are incorrectly assigned to operation.Summary instead of operation.Description. This causes the remarks to overwrite the summary that was set earlier in the code (line 374). The XML documentation model distinguishes between summary (brief description) and remarks (detailed notes), and they should map to different OpenAPI fields. This bug causes the loss of the summary information when remarks are present, resulting in incorrect OpenAPI documentation where remarks replace the intended summary.

groundTruth.title: Parameter lookup uses wrong identifier causing documentation mismatch
groundTruth.description: The code searches for operation parameters using propertyName instead of metadata.Name, which causes a mismatch when the property name differs from the parameter name in the API metadata. The metadata.Name represents how the parameter is actually named in the OpenAPI operation, while propertyName is the original C# property name. Using propertyName will fail to find the correct parameter in cases where parameter naming conventions differ (e.g., camelCase in API vs PascalCase in C#), resulting in parameter descriptions not being applied correctly to the OpenAPI documentation.

groundTruth.title: RequestBody description set after iteration may use stale or modified description value
groundTruth.description: The operation.RequestBody.Description is now set after iterating through media types and parsing examples, instead of before. This reordering creates a potential race condition or state dependency issue where the description variable could be modified or cleared during the example parsing logic. Additionally, if parsing throws an exception or causes early termination, the description will never be set. The original code correctly set the description first to ensure it's always applied before potentially failing example operations. This bug can result in missing or incorrect request body descriptions in the OpenAPI specification.

groundTruth.category: Test Methods Must Use Arrange-Act-Assert Pattern with Comments
groundTruth.title: Test method missing Arrange/Act/Assert comments
groundTruth.description: The new test method does not include the required '// Arrange', '// Act', and '// Assert' (or '// Act & Assert') comments to delineate phases per the AAA pattern.

groundTruth.category: Async Methods Must Be Named with Async Suffix
groundTruth.title: Async test method missing Async suffix
groundTruth.description: This method returns Task and is marked async, but its name does not end with the required 'Async' suffix.

groundTruth.category: All C# Source Files Must Include MIT License Header
groundTruth.title: New snapshot C# file missing MIT license header
groundTruth.description: Rule requires every .cs file to start with the exact two-line .NET Foundation MIT license header. This newly added snapshot file begins with an auto-generated header instead.

groundTruth.category: Use File-Scoped Namespace Declarations
groundTruth.title: New snapshot uses block-scoped namespaces instead of file-scoped
groundTruth.description: The file declares namespaces using block-scoped syntax with braces, violating the requirement to use file-scoped namespace declarations (ending with ';').

groundTruth.category: Use ArgumentNullException Throw Helpers for Parameter Validation
groundTruth.title: Manual null-check throws instead of using throw helper
groundTruth.description: Null parameter validation is performed via an explicit null check and throw. The rule requires using throw helpers such as ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(type).

groundTruth.category: Use ArgumentNullException Throw Helpers for Parameter Validation

producedFindings.title: Inconsistent namespace declaration syntax
producedFindings.description: The file changes from file-scoped namespace (line 16: `namespace Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.E2ETests.ServerExecutionTests;`) to block-scoped namespace with braces (lines 16-17 and closing brace at line 209). This creates inconsistency in the codebase style. The file now uses the older block-scoped syntax instead of the modern file-scoped namespace declaration.
producedFindings.recommendation: Either keep the file-scoped namespace syntax (remove the opening brace on line 17 and closing brace on line 209) or ensure this change aligns with the project's coding standards. If converting to block-scoped for a specific reason, document it.

producedFindings.title: Conditional logic added to error check without clear documentation
producedFindings.description: A new condition `AND '$(AspNetCorePatchVersion)' == '1'` is added to the BUILD002 error check. This means the error will only trigger for patch version 1, not for future servicing releases (patch versions 2, 3, etc.). This could allow breaking changes to slip through in future servicing updates.
producedFindings.recommendation: Clarify the intent: if this is meant to be a one-time exception for the 10.0.1 release, add a comment explaining why. If it should apply to all servicing releases, use a different condition like `'$(AspNetCorePatchVersion)' != '0'` instead.

producedFindings.title: ValidateBaseline changed from true to false without explanation
producedFindings.description: The `ValidateBaseline` property is changed from `true` to `false`. This disables baseline validation, which could allow unintended API or package changes to pass validation in this servicing release.
producedFindings.recommendation: Add a comment explaining why baseline validation is disabled for this release. Ensure this is intentional and temporary, and plan to re-enable it for the next release.

producedFindings.title: PreReleaseVersionLabel changed from 'rtm' to 'servicing'
producedFindings.description: The pre-release version label is changed from 'rtm' to 'servicing', which is appropriate for a servicing release (10.0.1). However, the PreReleaseBrandingLabel logic is now more complex with multiple conditional assignments. The 'servicing' label has no corresponding condition, so PreReleaseBrandingLabel will be empty for servicing releases.
producedFindings.recommendation: Add a condition for the 'servicing' label: `<PreReleaseBrandingLabel Condition="'$(PreReleaseVersionLabel)' == 'servicing'">Servicing $(PreReleaseVersionIteration)</PreReleaseBrandingLabel>` to ensure consistent branding.

producedFindings.title: MicroBuild output folder path changed without verification of downstream impact
producedFindings.description: The MicroBuild output folder is changed from `$(Build.SourcesDirectory)` to `$(Agent.TempDirectory)/MicroBuild`. The comment references a specific target `FindDotNetPathForMicroBuild` in arcade, but the change from `.dotnet` to `.dotnet-microbuild` for the SDK installation path (line 28) may have implications for the signing process that aren't fully documented in this diff.
producedFindings.recommendation: Verify that the arcade signing logic correctly handles the new `.dotnet-microbuild` path. Add a comment explaining why this specific directory structure is required and ensure all dependent build scripts are aware of this change.

groundTruth.category: Use File-Scoped Namespace Declarations
groundTruth.title: Traditional namespace declaration used
groundTruth.description: The rule requires C# files to use file-scoped namespace declarations (ending with semicolon without braces), but this file uses traditional namespace declarations with opening and closing braces.

groundTruth.title: XCode version mismatch with macOS image
groundTruth.description: The build pipeline configures macOS-15 image but attempts to use XCode 15.2.0 instead of the required XCode 16.4.0. The display name shows 'Use XCode 16.4.0' but the actual command selects XCode 15.2.0. This mismatch will cause build failures on macOS-15 runners since XCode 15.2.0 is not available on macOS-15 images, only on macOS-13. The PR originally upgraded both the macOS image and XCode version together, but this bug breaks that pairing.

groundTruth.title: Missing PreReleaseBrandingLabel for servicing builds
groundTruth.description: The PreReleaseBrandingLabel configuration is missing the condition for 'servicing' builds. While the PreReleaseVersionLabel is set to 'servicing', there is no corresponding conditional assignment for PreReleaseBrandingLabel when PreReleaseVersionLabel equals 'servicing'. This means servicing builds will have an undefined or incorrect branding label, causing incorrect version strings in build outputs and package metadata. The original PR added this condition to properly support servicing releases.

groundTruth.title: Inverted logic in servicing build validation
groundTruth.description: The condition for servicing build validation has inverted logic - it triggers an error when AspNetCorePatchVersion equals '1' instead of when it does not equal '1'. This means the first servicing release (patch version 1) will incorrectly fail builds if package references changed, while subsequent servicing releases (patch version 2, 3, etc.) will incorrectly allow breaking changes to package references. The original intent was to allow package reference changes only for the first servicing release (version 10.0.1) but block them for later releases.

producedFindings.title: Inconsistent namespace declaration syntax
producedFindings.description: The file changes from file-scoped namespace (line 16: `namespace Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.E2ETests.ServerExecutionTests;`) to block-scoped namespace with braces (lines 16-17 and closing brace at line 209). This creates inconsistency in the codebase style. The file now uses the older block-scoped syntax instead of the modern file-scoped namespace declaration.
producedFindings.recommendation: Either keep the file-scoped namespace syntax (remove the opening brace on line 17 and closing brace on line 209) or ensure this change aligns with the project's coding standards. If converting to block-scoped for a specific reason, document it.

producedFindings.title: ValidateBaseline changed from true to false without explanation
producedFindings.description: The `ValidateBaseline` property is changed from `true` to `false`. This disables baseline validation, which could allow unintended API or package changes to pass validation in this servicing release.
producedFindings.recommendation: Add a comment explaining why baseline validation is disabled for this release. Ensure this is intentional and temporary, and consider re-enabling it for future releases.

producedFindings.title: PreReleaseVersionLabel changed from 'rtm' to 'servicing'
producedFindings.description: The PreReleaseVersionLabel is changed from 'rtm' to 'servicing', which is appropriate for a servicing release (10.0.1). The 'servicing' label has no corresponding condition, so PreReleaseBrandingLabel will be empty for servicing releases.
producedFindings.recommendation: Add a condition for the 'servicing' label: `<PreReleaseBrandingLabel Condition="'$(PreReleaseVersionLabel)' == 'servicing'">Servicing $(PreReleaseVersionIteration)</PreReleaseBrandingLabel>` to ensure proper branding.

producedFindings.title: MicroBuild output folder path changed without verification of downstream impact
producedFindings.description: The MicroBuild output folder is changed from `$(Build.SourcesDirectory)` to `$(Agent.TempDirectory)/MicroBuild`. The comment references a specific target `FindDotNetPathForMicroBuild` in arcade, but the change from `.dotnet` to `.dotnet-microbuild` for the SDK installation path may have unintended consequences if other parts of the build system expect the old path.
producedFindings.recommendation: Verify that all references to the MicroBuild output folder and the .dotnet-microbuild SDK path have been updated throughout the build system. Test the signing process end-to-end to ensure it works correctly with the new paths.

groundTruth.title: Traditional namespace declaration used
groundTruth.description: The rule requires C# files to use file-scoped namespace declarations (ending with semicolon without braces), but this file uses traditional namespace declarations with opening and closing braces.

The original intent was to allow package reference changes only for the first servicing release (version 10.0.1) but block them for later releases.

producedFindings.title: Validation logic change in RetainedFileCountLimit setter
producedFindings.description: The validation condition changed from `if (value <= 0)` to `if (value < 0)`. This now allows `value == 0` to pass validation, whereas previously it would throw an exception. The documentation states 'strictly positive value' which means > 0, not >= 0. This creates a mismatch between the documentation and implementation.
producedFindings.recommendation: Either revert the condition to `if (value <= 0)` to match the 'strictly positive' documentation, or update the documentation to reflect that 0 is now allowed.

producedFindings.title: Validation logic change in BackgroundQueueSize setter
producedFindings.description: The validation condition changed from `if (value < 0)` to `if (value <= 0)`. This now rejects `value == 0` whereas previously it was allowed. The error message states 'must be non-negative' which includes 0, but the new condition rejects 0. This creates a mismatch between the error message and the validation logic.
producedFindings.recommendation: Either update the error message to say 'must be positive' or revert the condition to `if (value < 0)` to match the 'non-negative' message.

producedFindings.title: Variable initialization moved after loop in TryGetValidatableTypeInfo
producedFindings.description: The line `validatableTypeInfo = null;` was moved from after the loop (line 50 in original) to before the loop (line 55 in new). This changes the semantics: if a resolver returns true and sets validatableTypeInfo, the method now returns true with the correct value. However, the original code would have set it to null after the loop completed, which would be incorrect. The new placement is actually correct, but this is a significant behavioral change that could affect callers if they were relying on the buggy behavior.
producedFindings.recommendation: Verify that this change is intentional and that no code depends on the previous (incorrect) behavior where validatableTypeInfo would be null even when a resolver found a match.

producedFindings.title: Inconsistent formatting in method signature
producedFindings.description: The opening brace for TryGetValidatableParameterInfo method was moved from a new line to the same line as the method signature, changing from standard C# formatting convention used elsewhere in the codebase.
producedFindings.recommendation: Move the opening brace to a new line to maintain consistency with the rest of the codebase and C# style conventions.

groundTruth.category: Opening Braces Must Be on New Line (Allman Style)
groundTruth.title: Method brace on same line
groundTruth.description: The rule requires opening braces to appear on a new line after method declarations, but this method has its opening brace on the same line as the declaration (K&R style).

groundTruth.title: BackgroundQueueSize incorrectly rejects zero value despite 'non-negative' contract
groundTruth.description: The BackgroundQueueSize property validation was changed to reject values less than or equal to zero, but the error message states 'must be non-negative' which includes zero. According to the XML documentation, null represents no limit. While zero is semantically different from null, the validation is now inconsistent with the documented contract that explicitly states 'non-negative' (which mathematically includes zero). This prevents callers from setting a zero queue size if that was their intent, even though zero is a valid non-negative integer. The validation logic contradicts the error message, creating a confusing API contract.

groundTruth.title: RetainedFileCountLimit allows zero value violating positive-only constraint
groundTruth.description: The RetainedFileCountLimit property validation was changed from rejecting values <= 0 to only rejecting values < 0, which now allows zero as a valid value. However, both the XML documentation and the error message explicitly state that this must be a 'positive' value, and zero is not positive. The remarks indicate this controls maximum retained file count - a value of zero would mean no log files are retained, which could lead to complete log loss. This violates the documented API contract and creates a dangerous configuration scenario where all logs could be immediately discarded.

groundTruth.title: Premature null assignment in TryGetValidatableTypeInfo violates out parameter semantics
groundTruth.description: The validatableTypeInfo out parameter is now being set to null at the beginning of the method before iterating through resolvers, rather than after the loop when no resolver was found. While this may appear equivalent, it violates proper out parameter semantics and the contract implied by [NotNullWhen(true)]. If a resolver incorrectly returns true while leaving the out parameter unassigned or null (a buggy resolver implementation), the early null assignment would mask this contract violation. Additionally, this creates unnecessary assignment overhead since the parameter will be overwritten by successful resolvers. The original pattern of assigning null only when no resolver succeeds is more semantically correct and provides better detection of misbehaving resolvers.

groundTruth.category: Public APIs Must Have XML Documentation Comments
groundTruth.title: Empty <returns> XML documentation on public method
groundTruth.description: The public API method `AddAzureWebAppDiagnostics` includes a `<returns>` XML doc tag with no content. This results in incomplete XML documentation for a public API, violating the requirement that public APIs have complete XML documentation (including meaningful `<returns>` content when applicable).

If a resolver incorrectly returns true while leaving the out parameter unassigned or null (a buggy resolver implementation), the early null assignment would mask this contract violation. Additionally, this creates unnecessary assignment overhead since the parameter will be overwritten by successful resolvers. The original pattern of assigning null only when no resolver succeeds is more semantically correct and provides better detection of misbehaving resolvers.

groundTruth.category: Public APIs Must Have XML Documentation Comments
groundTruth.title: Empty <returns> XML documentation on public method
groundTruth.description: The public API method `AddAzureWebAppDiagnostics` includes a `<returns>` XML doc tag with no content. This results in incomplete XML documentation for a public API, violating the requirement that public APIs have complete XML documentation (including meaningful `<returns>` content when applicable).

producedFindings.title: orgId parameter accepted but not used in PBAC queries
producedFindings.description: The `getTeamIdsWithPermission` method accepts an `orgId` parameter but does not pass it to `getTeamIdsWithPermissions`. This means the organization scoping feature is broken for single-permission queries. The parameter is extracted from the destructured object but then ignored.
producedFindings.recommendation: Pass the `orgId` parameter through to the `getTeamIdsWithPermissions` call: `return this.getTeamIdsWithPermissions({ userId, permissions: [permission], fallbackRoles, orgId });`

producedFindings.title: Potential SQL injection in raw query with orgId parameter
producedFindings.description: The orgId parameter is used in a raw SQL query with template literal interpolation: `AND (${orgId}::bigint IS NULL OR t."id" = ${orgId} OR t."parentId" = ${orgId})`. While Prisma's `$queryRaw` does support parameterized queries, the current usage pattern with direct template interpolation is vulnerable if orgId is not properly validated. The parameter should be explicitly bound.
producedFindings.recommendation: Use Prisma's parameter binding syntax consistently. Consider using `Prisma.raw()` or ensure orgId is validated as a number before use. Alternatively, use conditional query building to avoid template interpolation of user input.

producedFindings.title: Inconsistent orgId scoping logic in PBAC queries
producedFindings.description: The fallback roles query includes `child."parentId" = ${orgId}` in the UNION clause (line 357), but the PBAC permissions query does not check `t."parentId" = ${orgId}` in the first part of the UNION. This creates asymmetric scoping logic where fallback roles may return different results than PBAC permissions for the same organization.
producedFindings.recommendation: Ensure both UNION branches apply the same organization scoping logic. The first branch should also check if the team's parent matches the orgId.

producedFindings.title: Removed organization scoping in booking handler without full replacement
producedFindings.description: The original code explicitly scoped memberships to `user.orgId` with an OR condition checking both direct team membership and parent organization membership. The refactored code passes `orgId: user.orgId ?? undefined` to the permission service, but the permission service's implementation may not handle the undefined case correctly for organization-scoped users. Additionally, the removed `getEventTypeIdsWhereUserIsAdminOrOwner` function is replaced with a simple call to `getEventTypeIdsFromTeamIdsFilter`, which may not properly handle the organization hierarchy.
producedFindings.recommendation: Verify that the PermissionCheckService correctly handles the orgId parameter when it's undefined vs when it's a number. Ensure the organization hierarchy scoping (checking both direct membership and parent organization) is preserved in the permission service implementation.

producedFindings.title: Test mocks incomplete for Kysely query builder
producedFindings.description: The mock Kysely query builder in the test file has incomplete method chaining. The `$if` method is mocked but the conditional logic it represents is not properly simulated. This could cause tests to pass when the actual code would fail due to missing conditional branches in the query building.
producedFindings.recommendation: Enhance the mock to properly simulate conditional query building. Consider using a more sophisticated mock library or creating a more complete mock implementation that tracks method calls and validates the query structure.

producedFindings.title: Missing validation of teamIds array in getUserIdsAndEmailsFromTeamIds
producedFindings.description: The function `getUserIdsAndEmailsFromTeamIds` returns early if `teamIds.length === 0`, but the calling code does not check for this condition before using the results. While the early return prevents errors, it's inconsistent with how `getEventTypeIdsFromTeamIdsFilter` handles empty arrays.
producedFindings.recommendation: Ensure consistent handling of empty arrays across all helper functions. Either all should return early with empty arrays, or all should handle empty input gracefully in the calling code.

producedFindings.title: Unused import in permission-check.service.ts
producedFindings.description: The `TRPCError` is imported but never used in the file. This suggests either incomplete refactoring or unnecessary import.
producedFindings.recommendation: Remove the unused import: `import { TRPCError } from "@trpc/server";`

producedFindings.title: Whitespace-only changes in permission-check.service.ts
producedFindings.description: Lines 140 and 204 contain whitespace-only changes (removing trailing whitespace). While not functionally problematic, these should be separated from functional changes or handled by a linter.
producedFindings.recommendation: Use a code formatter/linter to handle whitespace consistently. Separate formatting changes from functional changes in future PRs.

producedFindings.title: Test does not verify orgId parameter is passed correctly
producedFindings.description: The test `should call PermissionCheckService with correct parameters` does not verify that the `orgId` parameter is passed to the permission service. This means the test won't catch if the orgId parameter is accidentally omitted or incorrectly passed.
producedFindings.recommendation: Add assertion to verify orgId is passed: `expect(mockGetTeamIdsWithPermission).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.objectContaining({ orgId: mockUser.orgId ?? undefined }));`

groundTruth.category: Prevent Circular Dependencies Between Core Packages
groundTruth.title: Features package imports from trpc
groundTruth.description: The rule requires that the features package does not import from trpc, but permission-check.service.ts imports TRPCError from @trpc/server

groundTruth.title: orgId parameter not propagated in getTeamIdsWithPermission
groundTruth.description: The method getTeamIdsWithPermission accepts an orgId parameter to scope permission checks to a specific organization, but it fails to pass this parameter to the underlying getTeamIdsWithPermissions method. This causes organization scoping to be ignored when checking single permissions, allowing users to potentially access teams from all organizations instead of only the specified one. The bug breaks the core security feature introduced in this PR where organization-scoped permission checks were meant to restrict access based on orgId.

groundTruth.title: Incomplete orgId filtering in PBAC child team query
groundTruth.description: In the SQL query for getTeamsWithPBACPermissions, the WHERE clause for child teams is missing a check for child."parentId" = ${orgId}. The original PR added this condition to properly scope child teams when an orgId is provided, but this implementation only checks org."id" and child."id", missing the case where a child team's parent should match the orgId. This causes the query to incorrectly exclude child teams whose parentId matches the specified orgId, breaking the inheritance model where organization permissions should propagate to child teams.

groundTruth.title: Authorization bypass when filtering multiple userIds including current user
groundTruth.description: The isCurrentUser check was changed from a strict equality check (filters.userIds.length === 1 && user.id === filters.userIds[0]) to a loose inclusion check (filters.userIds.includes(user.id)). This allows a user to bypass permission checks by including their own userId in a list alongside other userIds they don't have permission to access. For example, if a user requests bookings for [ownUserId, unauthorizedUserId], the check passes even though they lack permission for the unauthorized user, allowing them to access bookings they shouldn't see.

groundTruth.category: Repository and Service Classes Must Follow Naming Conventions
groundTruth.title: Service file uses forbidden `.service.ts` suffix
groundTruth.description: Rule 1 forbids dot-suffix filenames like `.service.ts`. This service lives in `permission-check.service.ts` instead of a PascalCase `<Entity>Service.ts` filename matching the class name (e.g., `PermissionCheckService.ts`).

groundTruth.category: Repository and Service Classes Must Follow Naming Conventions
groundTruth.title: Repository does not follow `Prisma<Entity>Repository` naming convention
groundTruth.description: Rule 1 requires repository classes/files to follow the `Prisma<Entity>Repository.ts` pattern with matching exported class name. This repository is named `PermissionRepository` in `PermissionRepository.ts` (and not `PrismaPermissionRepository`).

groundTruth.category: Use Biome for Code Formatting with Standardized Configuration
groundTruth.title: Line exceeds configured 110-character width
groundTruth.description: Rule 3 requires Biome formatting with a 110 character line width. This newly added statement is formatted as a single long line exceeding that limit.

groundTruth.category: Use Biome for Code Formatting with Standardized Configuration
groundTruth.title: Import line exceeds configured 110-character width
groundTruth.description: Rule 3 requires Biome formatting with a 110 character line width. This import line is long enough to exceed the configured limit and should be wrapped/formatted accordingly.

producedFindings.title: orgId parameter accepted but not used in PBAC queries
producedFindings.description: The `getTeamIdsWithPermission` method accepts an `orgId` parameter but does not pass it to `getTeamIdsWithPermissions`. This means the organization scoping feature is broken for single-permission queries. The parameter is extracted from the destructured object but then ignored.
producedFindings.recommendation: Pass the `orgId` parameter through to the `getTeamIdsWithPermissions` call: `return this.getTeamIdsWithPermissions({ userId, permissions: [permission], fallbackRoles, orgId });`

producedFindings.title: Potential SQL injection in raw query with orgId parameter
producedFindings.description: The orgId parameter is used in a raw SQL query with template literal interpolation: `AND (${orgId}::bigint IS NULL OR t."id" = ${orgId} OR t."parentId" = ${orgId})`. While Prisma's `$queryRaw` does support parameterized queries, the current usage pattern with direct template interpolation is vulnerable if orgId is not properly validated. The parameter should be explicitly bound.
producedFindings.recommendation: Use Prisma's parameter binding syntax consistently. Consider using `Prisma.raw()` or ensure orgId is validated as a number before use. Alternatively, use conditional query building to avoid template interpolation of user input.

producedFindings.title: Inconsistent orgId scoping logic in PBAC queries
producedFindings.description: The orgId scoping condition in the fallback roles query includes `child."parentId" = ${orgId}` but the PBAC permissions query does not check `t."parentId" = ${orgId}` in the first part (only in the UNION). This inconsistency could lead to different results between the two query paths.
producedFindings.recommendation: Ensure both query paths (PBAC and fallback roles) apply the same orgId scoping logic consistently. Review the logic to confirm it correctly handles: (1) direct team membership, (2) child teams of the org, and (3) the org itself.

producedFindings.title: Removed organization scoping in booking handler without full replacement
producedFindings.description: The original code had explicit organization scoping logic: `...(user.orgId && { OR: [...] })` which ensured users could only access bookings within their organization. This logic is removed and replaced with a call to `permissionCheckService.getTeamIdsWithPermission()`, but the service is instantiated without any database context or caching, and the orgId is passed as `user.orgId ?? undefined`. If `user.orgId` is null/undefined, the permission check will return all teams the user has access to across all organizations, potentially exposing cross-organization data.
producedFindings.recommendation: Verify that the PermissionCheckService properly respects organization boundaries when orgId is undefined. If the service should only return teams within the user's organization, ensure orgId is always passed. Consider adding a guard: `orgId: user.orgId || undefined` with explicit validation that user.orgId exists when required.

producedFindings.title: Removed function getEventTypeIdsWhereUserIsAdminOrOwner without replacement
producedFindings.description: The function `getEventTypeIdsWhereUserIsAdminOrOwner` is removed and replaced with a direct call to `getEventTypeIdsFromTeamIdsFilter(prisma, teamIdsWithBookingPermission)`. However, the original function had specific logic to find event types where the user is admin/owner. The new code assumes that all event types in teams with booking permission should be included, which may be incorrect if event type-level permissions differ from team-level permissions.
producedFindings.recommendation: Verify that using `getEventTypeIdsFromTeamIdsFilter` with `teamIdsWithBookingPermission` produces the same results as the original logic. If event types have independent permission requirements, create a dedicated method or add event type filtering to the permission check.

producedFindings.title: PermissionCheckService instantiated without dependency injection
producedFindings.description: The PermissionCheckService is instantiated with `new PermissionCheckService()` without passing any dependencies. This assumes the service has a no-arg constructor and can initialize its own dependencies. This makes testing difficult and violates dependency injection principles. The service likely needs access to the database (prisma/kysely) which is not being provided.
producedFindings.recommendation: Pass the prisma client and other required dependencies to the PermissionCheckService constructor: `new PermissionCheckService(prisma, kysely)`. Update the service constructor to accept these dependencies.

producedFindings.title: Test mocks incomplete for PermissionCheckService
producedFindings.description: The test mocks PermissionCheckService but the mock implementation only provides `getTeamIdsWithPermission`. If the actual service has other methods or initialization logic that throws errors, the tests will not catch these issues. Additionally, the mock returns a function that returns the mocked method, but the actual service instantiation may fail if dependencies are required.
producedFindings.recommendation: Ensure the mock implementation matches the actual service interface completely. Add tests that verify the service is instantiated correctly with required dependencies. Consider using a more robust mocking library or creating a test double that more closely mirrors the real implementation.

producedFindings.title: Missing validation of teamIds array in getUserIdsAndEmailsFromTeamIds
producedFindings.description: The function `getUserIdsAndEmailsFromTeamIds` returns `[[], []]` when teamIds is empty, but this early return happens after the function is called. If `teamIdsWithBookingPermission` is empty, the function will return empty arrays, which is correct, but the calling code should handle this case explicitly to avoid confusion.
producedFindings.recommendation: Add a comment explaining the early return behavior, or consider moving the empty check to the caller to make the logic more explicit.

producedFindings.title: Integration test cleanup may fail silently
producedFindings.description: The integration tests perform cleanup by deleting teams and memberships, but if the test fails before cleanup, orphaned records may remain in the database. Additionally, there's no error handling if the cleanup itself fails.
producedFindings.recommendation: Use a try-finally block or a test fixture/teardown mechanism to ensure cleanup always runs. Consider using database transactions to automatically rollback test data.

producedFindings.title: Unused import in permission-check.service.ts
producedFindings.description: The import `import { TRPCError } from "@trpc/server";` is added but never used in the file. This suggests incomplete refactoring or leftover code.
producedFindings.recommendation: Remove the unused import: `import { TRPCError } from "@trpc/server";`

groundTruth.category: Prevent Circular Dependencies Between Core Packages
groundTruth.title: Features package imports from trpc
groundTruth.description: The rule requires that the features package does not import from trpc, but permission-check.service.ts imports TRPCError from @trpc/server

The original PR added this condition to properly scope child teams when an orgId is provided, but this implementation only checks org."id" and child."id", missing the case where a child team's parent should match the orgId. This allows a user to bypass permission checks by including their own userId in a list alongside other userIds they don't have permission to access. groundTruth.title: Service file uses forbidden `.service.ts` suffix
groundTruth.description: Rule 1 forbids dot-suffix filenames like `.service.ts`. This service lives in `permission-check.service.ts` instead of a PascalCase `<Entity>Service.ts` filename matching the class name (e.g., `PermissionCheckService.ts`).

groundTruth.category: Repository and Service Classes Must Follow Naming Conventions
groundTruth.title: Repository does not follow `Prisma<Entity>Repository` naming convention
groundTruth.description: Rule 1 requires repository classes/files to follow the `Prisma<Entity>Repository.ts` pattern with matching exported class name. This repository is named `PermissionRepository` in `PermissionRepository.ts` (and not `PrismaPermissionRepository`).

groundTruth.category: Use Biome for Code Formatting with Standardized Configuration
groundTruth.title: Line exceeds configured 110-character width
groundTruth.description: Rule 3 requires Biome formatting with a 110 character line width. This newly added statement is formatted as a single long line exceeding that limit.

groundTruth.category: Use Biome for Code Formatting with Standardized Configuration
groundTruth.title: Import line exceeds configured 110-character width
groundTruth.description: Rule 3 requires Biome formatting with a 110 character line width. This import line is long enough to exceed the configured limit and should be wrapped/formatted accordingly.

producedFindings.title: Inverted logic in hasPendingInviteByUserId - checks for accepted instead of pending
producedFindings.description: The new static method `hasPendingInviteByUserId` checks for `accepted: true` when it should check for `accepted: false`. This inverts the logic and causes the method to return true for accepted invites and false for pending invites, which is the opposite of what the method name and callers expect. This will break the onboarding redirect logic that depends on detecting pending invites.
producedFindings.recommendation: Change `accepted: true` to `accepted: false` on line 588 to correctly identify pending (unaccepted) invites.

producedFindings.title: Logic condition changed from AND to OR without clear justification
producedFindings.description: The condition on line 69 was changed from `if (pendingInvite && onboardingV3Enabled)` to `if (hasPendingInvite || onboardingV3Enabled)`. This changes the behavior from requiring both conditions to be true to requiring either one. This means users will now be redirected to personal settings onboarding whenever onboarding-v3 is enabled, regardless of pending invites, which may not be the intended behavior.
producedFindings.recommendation: Verify the intended logic. If the original AND condition was correct, revert to `if (hasPendingInvite && onboardingV3Enabled)`. If OR is intentional, add a comment explaining why the behavior changed.

producedFindings.title: Integration test may have flaky assertions
producedFindings.description: In the test 'should return true when user has both accepted and pending invites', the assertion `expect(result).toBe(team2 ? true : false)` will pass even if `team2` is not found, making the test less reliable. The test should either ensure a second team exists or skip the test if one cannot be found.
producedFindings.recommendation: Either create a second test team in the beforeAll hook, or skip this test case if a second team cannot be found. Alternatively, create a new team specifically for this test.

groundTruth.category: Use Biome for Code Formatting with Standardized Configuration
groundTruth.title: Single quotes instead of double quotes
groundTruth.description: The rule requires using double quotes for all strings in TypeScript/JavaScript files, but single quotes were used for the empty string assignment.

groundTruth.title: Logic inversion in hasPendingInviteByUserId method
groundTruth.description: The hasPendingInviteByUserId method checks for accepted:true memberships instead of accepted:false (pending invites). This inverts the method's logic - it will return true when users have accepted team memberships rather than pending invites. This breaks the onboarding flow for users with team invites, causing users with accepted memberships to be redirected to personal onboarding instead of users with pending invites. The method name and its intended purpose clearly indicate it should check for pending (non-accepted) invites.

groundTruth.title: Incorrect conditional operator in onboarding redirect logic
groundTruth.description: The conditional logic for redirecting users with pending invites uses OR (||) instead of AND (&&). This means users will be redirected to '/onboarding/personal/settings' if they have a pending invite OR if onboarding-v3 is enabled, rather than requiring both conditions. This breaks the normal onboarding flow - any user with onboarding-v3 enabled will be incorrectly redirected to personal settings regardless of whether they have pending invites. The intended behavior was to only redirect users who have both onboarding-v3 enabled AND a pending invite.

groundTruth.category: Use Biome for Code Formatting with Standardized Configuration
groundTruth.title: Line exceeds 110 character width
groundTruth.description: Biome formatting requires a max line width of 110 characters. This newly added line appears to exceed that limit and should be wrapped across multiple lines.
